Title: Card Number Iframe Isolation
Preconditions:
- CyberSource payment form displayed
- Browser DevTools open
Steps:
1. Open DevTools Elements tab
2. Navigate to card number input
3. Identify parent iframe
4. Check iframe src domain
5. Verify testflex.cybersource.com
6. Try: document.querySelector('iframe').contentDocument
Expected Result:
- Card field hosted in iframe
- Domain: testflex.cybersource.com
- Cross-origin blocks access
- DOMException on access attempt

Title: CVV Iframe Isolation
Steps:
1. Inspect CVV/security code field
2. Verify iframe implementation
3. Check sandbox attributes
4. Attempt JavaScript access
Expected Result:
- CVV in secure iframe
- Proper sandboxing
- Cannot access from parent page

Title: CVV Visual Masking
Steps:
1. Enter CVV: 838
2. Observe field display
3. Check if digits visible
4. Verify masking
Expected Result:
- CVV displayed as •••
- Actual digits never visible
- Shoulder-surfing prevented

Title: No Card Data in Network Requests
Preconditions:
- DevTools Network tab open
- CyberSource form filled
Steps:
1. Fill form with test card data
2. Submit order
3. Wait for all requests to complete
4. Search all requests for '4622943127013705'
5. Search all requests for '838'
6. Check request payloads
Expected Result:
- No raw card number in any request
- No CVV in any request
- Only tokenized data sent
- CyberSource handles all card data

Title: No Card Data in Browser Storage
Steps:
1. Open DevTools Application tab
2. Check localStorage for card data
3. Check sessionStorage
4. Check cookies
5. Search for partial card number
Expected Result:
- No card numbers stored
- No CVV stored anywhere
- Only safe data persisted

Title: HTTPS Enforcement
Steps:
1. Check browser URL bar (https)
2. Inspect iframe sources
3. Check Network tab for http requests
4. Verify no mixed content warnings
Expected Result:
- All connections HTTPS
- Iframes use HTTPS
- No mixed content
- Secure padlock shown

Title: JWT Token Authentication
Steps:
1. Inspect iframe src attributes
2. Look for JWT parameter
3. Verify token format (xxx.yyy.zzz)
4. Note token uniqueness
Expected Result:
- JWT present in iframe URL
- Valid three-part structure
- Token unique per session

Title: Session Token Security
Steps:
1. Check for access token storage
2. Verify HttpOnly cookies where applicable
3. Check token expiration
4. Verify refresh mechanism
Expected Result:
- Tokens stored securely
- HttpOnly for sensitive cookies
- Reasonable expiration
- Refresh works

Title: Logout Token Revocation
Steps:
1. Note current access token
2. Click logout
3. Check /revoke endpoint called
4. Attempt to use old token
5. Verify rejection
Expected Result:
- Logout revokes token
- Old token rejected
- Cannot reuse after logout

Title: Account Lockout Protection
Steps:
1. Attempt login with wrong password
2. Repeat 5 times
3. Verify lockout message
4. Attempt with correct password
5. Verify still locked
Expected Result:
- Account locks after 5 failures
- Clear lockout message
- Correct password also rejected
- Lockout has timeout

Title: XSS Prevention - Search
Steps:
1. Enter: <script>alert('XSS')</script>
2. Execute search
3. Verify no script execution
4. Check results display
Expected Result:
- Script not executed
- Input escaped/sanitized
- Safe display of results

Title: XSS Prevention - Forms
Steps:
1. Enter XSS payload in name field
2. Submit form
3. View saved data
4. Verify no execution
Expected Result:
- Input sanitized
- No script execution
- Safe rendering

Title: SQL Injection Prevention
Steps:
1. Enter: ' OR '1'='1
2. Execute search
3. Verify normal handling
4. Check for error messages
Expected Result:
- Query handled safely
- No SQL errors exposed
- Normal results or empty

Title: Path Traversal Prevention
Steps:
1. Attempt ../../../etc/passwd in URLs
2. Attempt in file names
3. Verify rejection
Expected Result:
- Path traversal blocked
- Proper input validation
- No sensitive file access

Title: CSRF Token Validation
Steps:
1. Inspect form for CSRF token
2. Verify token in requests
3. Attempt to forge request without token
Expected Result:
- CSRF tokens present
- Server validates tokens
- Forged requests rejected

Title: CORS Configuration
Steps:
1. Make API request
2. Check CORS headers
3. Verify allowed origins
Expected Result:
- Proper CORS headers
- Origins restricted appropriately
- Credentials handled correctly

Title: No Credentials in Logs
Steps:
1. Check browser console
2. Check Network responses
3. Verify no passwords logged
Expected Result:
- No passwords in console
- No sensitive data in responses
- Error messages sanitized

Title: Error Message Safety
Steps:
1. Trigger authentication errors
2. Trigger validation errors
3. Check error messages
Expected Result:
- No stack traces exposed
- No internal paths shown
- Generic safe messages

Title: Session expiry during checkout — re-auth preserves cart
Preconditions:
- Customer logged in with items in cart
- Checkout flow started (shipping address entered)
- Token expiry mechanism available (short-lived token or manual invalidation)
Steps:
1. Log in as customer and add products to cart
2. Proceed to checkout and enter shipping address
3. Wait for session token to expire (or manually invalidate token via API/DevTools)
4. Attempt to proceed to payment step
5. Verify system detects expired session and prompts for re-authentication
6. Re-authenticate with same customer credentials
7. Verify cart contents are intact after re-authentication
8. Verify checkout can resume from shipping/payment step
9. Execute xAPI cart query to confirm all line items and quantities preserved
Expected Result:
1. Expired session is detected when attempting checkout action
2. User is prompted to re-authenticate (redirect to login or modal)
3. After re-auth, cart contents are fully intact (same items, quantities, prices)
4. Checkout resumes without requiring user to re-enter shipping address
5. No data loss during session expiry and re-authentication flow

Title: Session re-auth during checkout — resume from last step
Preconditions:
- Customer logged in with items in cart
- Checkout flow in progress (shipping address and method selected)
- Token expiry mechanism available
Steps:
1. Log in as customer and add products to cart
2. Proceed through checkout: enter shipping address, select shipping method
3. Arrive at payment step
4. Force session expiry (clear auth token from storage or wait for expiry)
5. Attempt to submit payment or interact with payment form
6. Verify re-authentication prompt appears
7. Re-authenticate with same credentials
8. Verify checkout state: shipping address still populated, shipping method still selected
9. Verify payment step is accessible without restarting checkout
10. Complete the order successfully
Expected Result:
1. Re-authentication prompt appears on expired session during payment
2. After re-auth, shipping address is still populated (not cleared)
3. Shipping method selection is preserved
4. User lands back on payment step (not restarted to beginning)
5. Order can be completed successfully after session re-auth
6. Checkout state is fully preserved across session boundary

Title: Session expiry does not create partial or orphan order
Preconditions:
- Customer logged in with items in cart
- Checkout flow started
- xAPI access available for order verification
- Token expiry mechanism available
Steps:
1. Log in as customer and add products to cart
2. Proceed to checkout and enter all required information
3. Force session expiry immediately before order submission (clear token)
4. Attempt to submit/place the order with expired token
5. Verify order submission fails gracefully (not silently)
6. Re-authenticate with same credentials
7. Execute xAPI orders query to check for any new orders:
   query { orders(storeId: "${STORE_ID}", cultureName: "en-US") { totalCount items { number status } } }
8. Verify no partial or orphan order was created during the failed submission
9. Verify cart still contains the original items
10. Complete the order normally after re-auth
Expected Result:
1. Order submission with expired token fails with clear error (not silent)
2. No partial order exists in the system (no orphan order with incomplete data)
3. No payment was captured or authorized for the failed attempt
4. Cart remains intact with all items after session expiry
5. Order can be completed normally after re-authentication
6. System maintains transactional integrity — no order created until full completion
